mnemonics: SELECT JOINS WHERE FUNCTIONS GROUP; Think: 'A careful JOIN selects functions for the group.' to remember the logical order and key components of complex queries.
keyPoints: Master different JOIN types (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL) to combine data from multiple tables.
Use aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN) with GROUP BY to summarize data.
Filter grouped results using the HAVING clause, not the WHERE clause.
Utilize subqueries to break down complex problems into simpler steps.
Understand the logical query processing order (FROM, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, SELECT, ORDER BY).
commonMistakes: Using WHERE instead of HAVING to filter aggregated data.
Forgetting to include all non-aggregated columns in the GROUP BY clause.
Creating ambiguous column names in joins by not using table aliases.
Mishandling NULL values in aggregate functions and conditional logic.
Writing inefficient queries with unnecessary subqueries that could be replaced with JOINs.
